summary: The synthetic bridge packet correctly enforces boundary discipline and opaque source semantics, but requires follow-up to ensure finding keys remain stable across runs.

findings.title: Verify finding key stability across runs
findings.summary: Evidence C suggests stable finding keys are ready, but the caveat notes that not all findings are equally portable; ensure all keys remain generic.

findings.title: Clarify authority gap in bridge template
findings.summary: Evidence B indicates a need for clearer explanation of caller-owned authority; tighten the public-safe bridge packet template.

findings.title: Ensure no-write boundary is explicit
findings.summary: Evidence D confirms the bridge output must remain advisory; verify this constraint is consistently applied in downstream templates.

decision.summary: The synthetic bridge packet correctly enforces boundary discipline and opaque source semantics, but requires follow-up to ensure finding keys remain stable across runs.

non_blocking_findings.title: Verify finding key stability across runs
non_blocking_findings.summary: Evidence C suggests stable finding keys are ready, but the caveat notes that not all findings are equally portable; ensure all keys remain generic.

non_blocking_findings.title: Clarify authority gap in bridge template
non_blocking_findings.summary: Evidence B indicates a need for clearer explanation of caller-owned authority; tighten the public-safe bridge packet template.

non_blocking_findings.title: Ensure no-write boundary is explicit
non_blocking_findings.summary: Evidence D confirms the bridge output must remain advisory; verify this constraint is consistently applied in downstream templates.

follow_up: Validate that all finding keys in the packet remain generic and do not encode private naming or caller-owned identifiers.
Review the bridge packet template to ensure the authority note explicitly states that downstream routing and writes remain caller-owned.
